root_text.sn51.77-86:1.5: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ uddhambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya cattāro iddhipādā bhāvetabbā.
root_text.sn51.77-86:1.7: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu chandasamādhippadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti,
root_text.sn51.77-86:1.11: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ uddhambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya ime cattāro iddhipādā bhāvetabbā”ti.

translation_text.sn51.77-86:1.3: Desire for rebirth in the realm of luminous form, desire for rebirth in the formless realm, conceit, restlessness, and ignorance.
translation_text.sn51.77-86:1.4: These are the five higher fetters.
translation_text.sn51.77-86:1.5: The four bases of psychic power should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these five higher fetters.
translation_text.sn51.77-86:1.7: It’s when a mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm …
translation_text.sn51.77-86:1.11: These four bases of psychic power should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these five higher fetters.”
translation_text.sn51.77-86:1.12: (Tell in full as in the Linked Discourses on the Path at SN 45.171–180.)
translation_text.sn51.77-86:2.4: aggregates, and fetters high and low.
translation_text.sn51.77-86:2.5: The Linked Discourses on the Bases of Psychic Power is the seventh section.
